title: lupinus polyphyllus var. pallidipes
intro: Lupinus polyphyllus var. pallidipes, also known as the pale-spurred lupine or white-spurred lupine, is a variant of the perennial lupine plant native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant's roots and flowers have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Lupinus polyphyllus var. pallidipes include reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, as well as supporting the immune system and acting as a natural antioxidant. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and flowers can be dried and made into teas, tinctures, or infusions to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
